# branch edit --no-restack leaves upstack branches untouched.

as 'Test <test@example.com>'
at '2024-05-27T13:57:09Z'

cd repo
git init
git commit --allow-empty -m 'Initial commit'
gs repo init

git add feature1.txt
gs branch create feature1 -m 'Add feature 1'
git add feature2.txt
gs branch create feature2 -m 'Add feature 2'

git rev-parse --short feature2
cmp stdout $WORK/golden/feature2-before.txt

gs down

# Rewrite feature1 so an automatic restack would necessarily rewrite feature2.
env EDITOR=mockedit MOCKEDIT_GIVE=$WORK/input/rebase-todo.txt
gs branch edit --no-restack

# The original feature2 head proves that the rewritten feature1 was not
# propagated upstack.
git rev-parse --short feature2
cmp stdout $WORK/golden/feature2-before.txt

-- repo/feature1.txt --
Feature 1
-- repo/feature2.txt --
Feature 2
-- input/rebase-todo.txt --
pick 5c87b6d Add feature 1
exec git commit --amend -m 'Edit feature 1'
-- golden/feature2-before.txt --
6afdaad
